project_overview.project_development_objective: To respond promptly and effectively to an eligible crisis or emergency in Djibouti
project_overview.total_financing: Up to 10 percent of undisbursed balances in existing IPF and PforR projects per fiscal year (amount defined at activation)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Entire population including refugees, migrants, IDPs, poor and vulnerable households, rural communities, small-scale farmers, livestock herders, urban populations affected by flooding

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - hosts 32,000 refugees and asylum seekers from Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Yemen, plus 150,000 non-registered migrants; located in restive Horn of Africa region

key_components.description: Procurement and distribution of water, food, agricultural inputs, emergency response supplies, medicines, hygiene kits, medical equipment, veterinary medicine, water pumps, school supplies
Incremental operational expenditures, overtime for government officials, mobilization costs, PIU functioning costs, technical assistance

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Economy and Finance in charge of Industry (MEFI) through Africa Disaster Risk Financing Programme (ADRiFi) Project Implementation Unit
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Executive Secretariat for Disaster Risk Management (SEGRC), Interministerial Technical Committee for Disaster Risk Reduction, Regional Committees for DRM
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Fast-track procedures under Paragraph 12 of IPF Policy, Direct Selection or Request for Quotations above current thresholds, Framework Agreements
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: CERP PIU responsible for M&E with regular reporting to World Bank, Results Framework tracking

identified_risks.risk_description: Governance arrangements spread across various ministries with unclear mandates and financing for disaster risk management
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Bank-executed technical assistance to clarify procedures and attribution between stakeholders, specify national procedures in activation process

identified_risks.risk_description: PIU working with Bank for first time, limited capacity of national authorities like SEGRC due to lack of funding and staff
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building support through simulation workshops, leverage existing Bank PIUs in country

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential delays in procurement planning and processing, unavailability of implementation teams during emergencies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Proactive and adaptable procurement processing, flexibility in national institutional review requirements, leverage external support from active projects

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited number of national level strategies, sectoral policies, and crisis-specific plans
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Support crisis planning at national level through technical assistance, increase preparedness and response capacity

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Accountability Mechanism available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender-disaggregated indicators included, particular emphasis on women in rural communities

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to work in stable country within restive region, coordination with national DRM framework to avoid duplication and ensure coordinated response
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Located in Horn of Africa prone to conflict, hosts significant refugee and migrant populations, vulnerable to regional climate shocks and political instability
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Small state designation, hands-on expanded implementation support required, security risks from regional instability affecting migration flows
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - hosts approximately 32,000 refugees and asylum seekers from Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Yemen (3% of population), plus 150,000 non-registered migrants; 16,000 stranded migrants arrived in 2023
